name: Carrot
description: Carrots are packed with beta-carotene, which your body turns into vitamin A for healthy eyes.

name: Tomato
description: Tomatoes contain lycopene — an antioxidant linked to heart health. Bonus: they ripen better off the vine at room temp.

name: Corn
description: Corn is the most-grown crop on Earth — used for food, fuel (ethanol), and animal feed.

name: Strawberry
description: A single strawberry has about 200 tiny seeds on the outside. They're surprisingly rich in vitamin C!

name: Potato
description: Potatoes produce more calories per acre than any major grain, making them a sustainability superstar.

name: Broccoli
description: Broccoli is loaded with sulforaphane, a compound studied for its cancer-fighting potential.

name: Bell Pepper
description: Red, yellow, and green peppers are the same plant at different ripeness — red has the most vitamin C.

name: Avocado
description: Avocados are nutrient-dense but water-hungry — one fruit can take ~200L of water to grow.

name: Apple Tree
description: An apple tree can produce fruit for 50+ years. Keeps doctors away — and absorbs a lot of CO₂.

name: Pineapple
description: Pineapples take up to 2 years to grow! Each plant usually yields only one fruit at a time.

name: Mushroom
description: Mushrooms aren't plants — they're fungi that grow on decomposing matter, recycling nutrients.

name: Grape Vine
description: Grape vines can live over 100 years. Older vines often produce the most flavorful fruit.
